title: crispy sweet jamaican plantains hash
description: nutty, shaggy, ovo-vegetarian-friendly breakfast dish featuring boil, jam, and crispy plantains
instructions: Boil the plantains in salted water for 15-20 minutes, then drain.
Cut the boiled plantains into 1-inch pieces and set aside.
Heat the melted butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
Dust the plantains with flour and cook until golden, about 5 minutes.
Add the grated coconut, jerk seasoning, and brown sugar to the skillet and stir.
Cook for 2-3 minutes, until the mixture is fragrant and toasted.
Add the beaten eggs to the skillet and stir to combine.
Cook for 2-3 minutes, until the eggs are set.
Transfer the plantain mixture to a serving dish and top with shredded cheese, if using.
Return the skillet to the stovetop and add the apple jelly.
Cook for 1-2 minutes, until the jelly is melted and the mixture is well combined.
Pour the jelly mixture over the plantain mixture and serve hot, garnished with chopped scallions.
